extra.reasoning:
chest_pain appears in only one rule: [interaction_drop, priority 100] IF (palpitations and rash and chest_pain) THEN acuity := self_care. This rule has the dominant priority (100, far above any other), and it sets acuity to the LOWEST level (self_care).

Counterexample (the witness is the chest_pain=true state): palpitations=true, rash=true, chest_pain=true, dizziness=false, focal_weakness=false, hemoptysis=false.

BEFORE (chest_pain=false, all else identical): matching rules are raise_rash (pri 3 -> emergent) and raise_palpitations (pri 2 -> urgent). interaction_drop does not fire (needs chest_pain). Highest priority wins: raise_rash, so acuity = emergent.

AFTER (chest_pain=true): raise_rash (pri 3 -> emergent), raise_palpitations (pri 2 -> urgent), and now interaction_drop (pri 100 -> self_care) fires because all three of palpitations, rash, chest_pain are true. The highest priority matching rule is interaction_drop (100), so acuity = self_care.

Enum order is self_care < routine < urgent < emergent. Setting chest_pain true drops acuity from emergent to self_care, the maximum possible decrease. This violates the monotonicity property that setting chest_pain true must never lower acuity.
